title: Shahpur Kandi Dam Reaches Target Operational Date — Ravi Waters Diverted from Pakistan
detail: March 31, 2026 marked the target operational date for India's Shahpur Kandi Dam on the Ravi River, confirmed by J&K Jal Shakti Minister Javed Ahmed Rana in February 2026. The 55.5-metre dam (206 MW) in Pathankot, Punjab, diverts surplus Ravi River waters to irrigate over 37,000 hectares in Kathua and Samba districts of J&K — waters that previously flowed unused into Pakistan. The dam's operationalization is a direct consequence of India's April 2025 suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty. Under the IWT, India had historically left much of the Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej flow unused, sending it downstream to Pakistan; the IWT suspension removed that constraint. Pakistan had previously stated that any interruption of Ravi River flows 'would be considered acts of war,' and referred the IWT suspension to the Permanent Court of Arbitration, which India rejected as outside the tribunal's jurisdiction.

title: SATP Analysis: 90 Militants Remain Active in J&K; Only One LOC Violation in 2026
detail: A Eurasia Review security analysis published March 31, 2026, citing South Asia Terrorism Portal (SATP) data, assessed that approximately 90 terrorists remain active in Jammu & Kashmir: roughly 40–50 in the Jammu region and 40 in the Kashmir Valley. The data indicated at least 10 terrorist and associate fatalities in J&K year-to-date as of March 28, 2026 — a lower tempo than previous years. Significantly, only one LOC ceasefire violation was recorded in all of 2026 (February 20, Kupwara/Nowgam sector), a sharp contrast with 5,133 violations in 2020 and ~219 in 2024. The February 2021 ceasefire agreement between DGMOs of India and Pakistan along the LOC has broadly held despite the diplomatic deterioration following Operation Sindoor.

title: BASIC Report: May 2025 India-Pakistan Crisis Most Severe Since 1971
detail: The British American Security Information Council (BASIC) published a detailed analysis on March 31, 2026 describing the May 2025 India-Pakistan military confrontation (Operation Sindoor and Pakistan's retaliatory exchanges) as the most severe military confrontation between the two nuclear-armed nations since the 1971 war. The analysis assessed that the crisis came closer to nuclear use than any previous incident, with both sides deploying nuclear-capable delivery systems to alert status. BASIC noted that US President Trump's direct intervention with both Prime Minister Modi and Pakistan's Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir was pivotal in engineering a ceasefire on May 10, 2025. The report called for renewed efforts to establish nuclear risk reduction measures between India and Pakistan, including hotlines and pre-notification protocols for missile tests.
